questions.prompt: Which research method establishes cause-and-effect by manipulating an independent variable while controlling for confounds?
questions.topic: Unit 0: Science of Psychology — Research Methods

questions.prompt: A researcher randomly assigns participants to two groups. This procedure primarily helps to:
choices: Eliminate the need for a control group

questions.prompt: A correlation coefficient of -0.85 indicates:
The DSM-5 is primarily used to:
Which ethical principle requires that participants be told the true nature of a study after it ends?
Which neurotransmitter is most associated with the brain's reward pathway and is implicated in addiction?
Damage to which brain structure most directly impairs the formation of new long-term memories?
The fight-or-flight response is primarily controlled by which branch of the autonomic nervous system?
Phineas Gage's personality change after his accident provided early evidence for the function of which brain region?
Which neurotransmitter is most associated with mood regulation and is the target of SSRI antidepressants?
The all-or-none principle states that a neuron will:
choices: Fire at varying strengths depending on input
Fire only when threshold is crossed, always at the same intensity
Fire only in response to inhibitory signals
Never fire if any inhibitory input is present
questions.correctText: Fire only when threshold is crossed, always at the same intensity

questions.prompt: Which lobe of the brain is most responsible for processing visual information?
Broca's area, located in the frontal lobe, is primarily involved in:
Identical twins raised apart who show similar IQ scores provide evidence for which influence on intelligence?
The endocrine system communicates via:
choices: Reflex arcs in the spinal cord

questions.prompt: REM sleep is characterized by:
choices: Deep relaxation and slow brain waves
Vivid dreaming and rapid eye movement
The first stage of falling asleep
questions.correctText: Vivid dreaming and rapid eye movement

questions.prompt: Which psychologist's experiments with dogs and bells established classical conditioning?
In Pavlov's experiment, the meat powder is the:
A child stops touching a hot stove after burning themselves once. This is an example of:
Reinforcement schedules that produce the highest, most consistent rate of responding are:
Albert Bandura's Bobo doll experiment demonstrated:
Information held in short-term memory typically lasts how long without rehearsal?
George Miller's research showed that short-term memory capacity is approximately:
Memorizing a phone number by grouping digits is an example of:
The misinformation effect, demonstrated by Elizabeth Loftus, shows that:
choices: Eyewitness memory can be altered by leading questions
Memory is perfectly stored like a recording
questions.correctText: Eyewitness memory can be altered by leading questions

questions.prompt: Procedural memory is best illustrated by:
The tip-of-the-tongue phenomenon is an example of:
Which thinking error involves searching only for information that confirms what you already believe?
Estimating the frequency of an event based on how easily examples come to mind is the:
Solving a problem suddenly without conscious step-by-step reasoning is called:
Linguistic relativity (the Whorfian hypothesis) suggests that:
choices: All humans share identical language structures
Language is innate and learned without exposure
The language we speak influences how we think
Animals can be taught full human language
questions.correctText: The language we speak influences how we think

questions.prompt: Piaget's stage in which children develop object permanence is:
Erikson identified the primary developmental task of adolescence as:
Harry Harlow's monkey experiments demonstrated that infants attach most strongly to caregivers who provide:
An authoritative parenting style is characterized by:
Kohlberg's preconventional level of moral reasoning is primarily based on:
Which sensory threshold is the smallest difference between two stimuli that can be detected 50% of the time?
Color blindness most commonly results from a problem with:
The phenomenon by which familiar objects appear the same size regardless of viewing distance is called:
Gestalt psychologists emphasized that:
choices: Behavior can only be measured through reflexes
The whole is greater than the sum of the parts
Mental processes can never be studied scientifically
questions.correctText: The whole is greater than the sum of the parts

questions.prompt: Top-down processing relies primarily on:
The McGurk effect demonstrates that:
choices: Vision and hearing are processed independently
Visual cues influence what we hear
questions.correctText: Visual cues influence what we hear

questions.prompt: Spearman's 'g' refers to:
choices: General intelligence underlying all mental abilities
questions.correctText: General intelligence underlying all mental abilities

questions.prompt: An IQ test's reliability refers to:
choices: Whether it measures what it claims to measure
How consistent its scores are across administrations
Whether the test is fair across cultures
How quickly the test can be administered
questions.correctText: How consistent its scores are across administrations

questions.prompt: Maslow's hierarchy of needs places which need at the top?
Drive-reduction theory explains motivation as the body's attempt to:
The James-Lange theory of emotion proposes that:
choices: We feel emotion first, then the body responds
Body response and emotion happen simultaneously
We notice the body's response and then experience the emotion
questions.correctText: We notice the body's response and then experience the emotion

questions.prompt: The two-factor theory of emotion (Schachter-Singer) requires:
choices: Both physiological arousal AND cognitive labeling
questions.correctText: Both physiological arousal AND cognitive labeling

questions.prompt: Selye's general adaptation syndrome stages are:
Freud's defense mechanism in which unacceptable impulses are pushed out of conscious awareness is called:
Carl Rogers's person-centered therapy emphasizes:
The Big Five personality model includes which trait?
An individual with a strong internal locus of control most likely believes that:
choices: Luck and fate determine their outcomes
Their own effort determines their outcomes
questions.correctText: Their own effort determines their outcomes

questions.prompt: Bandura's concept of self-efficacy refers to:
choices: One's belief in one's own ability to succeed
The unconscious mind's influence on behavior
questions.correctText: One's belief in one's own ability to succeed

questions.prompt: Hallucinations and delusions are positive symptoms most associated with:
Persistent, intrusive thoughts paired with repetitive behaviors aimed at reducing anxiety characterize:
Bipolar disorder is characterized by:
choices: Alternating episodes of mania and depression
questions.correctText: Alternating episodes of mania and depression

questions.prompt: A panic attack is most associated with which class of disorder?
Cognitive therapy for depression (Aaron Beck) primarily focuses on:
Systematic desensitization is a behavior therapy primarily used to treat:
Antipsychotic medications primarily target which neurotransmitter system?
SSRIs increase the availability of:
Solomon Asch's conformity studies showed that participants conformed to incorrect group answers about:
choices: 75% of the time on every trial
Roughly one third of trials on average
Never, when the answer was obvious
Only when the group was very small
questions.correctText: Roughly one third of trials on average

questions.prompt: Stanley Milgram's obedience study found that approximately what percentage of participants delivered the maximum shock?
The bystander effect is best explained by:
Cognitive dissonance theory predicts that people change their attitudes to:
choices: Avoid all forms of mental effort

questions.prompt: The fundamental attribution error involves:
choices: Overestimating situational factors in others' behavior
Overestimating dispositional (personality) factors in others' behavior
Always blaming oneself for negative events
Refusing to make any judgments about behavior
questions.correctText: Overestimating dispositional (personality) factors in others' behavior

questions.prompt: Stereotype threat refers to:
choices: The threat of being attacked due to one's identity
Underperformance caused by awareness of a negative stereotype about one's group
An increase in performance when stereotyped positively
Loss of cultural identity through assimilation
questions.correctText: Underperformance caused by awareness of a negative stereotype about one's group

questions.prompt: In-group bias is the tendency to:
choices: Favor members of one's own group over outsiders
questions.correctText: Favor members of one's own group over outsiders

questions.prompt: Zimbardo's Stanford Prison Experiment is often cited as evidence for:
choices: The power of social roles and situations to shape behavior
questions.correctText: The power of social roles and situations to shape behavior

questions.prompt: Robert Cialdini's principles of social influence include:
Group polarization typically results in:
choices: The majority adopting the minority view
Group decisions that are more extreme than initial individual opinions
questions.correctText: Group decisions that are more extreme than initial individual opinions

questions.prompt: Groupthink is most likely when:
choices: Group members have diverse opinions and challenge each other openly
A group is highly cohesive and isolated from outside perspectives
A group includes outside consultants and devil's advocates
Members are anonymous and don't know each other
questions.correctText: A group is highly cohesive and isolated from outside perspectives
